task: Starting from publicly available datasets, propose a not-thought-of-yet or new compound to treat complex-I mitochondrial diseases.

method.approach: Quantitative candidate-node ranking (genetic-modifier evidence -> pharmacology) cross-checked against patient/model dysregulation, tractability, and tissue access; then a five-rung causal ladder from screen signal to a specific compound; a tissue-access/exposure gate; and a documented novelty search. Scoring rubric and rules fixed before compound identity was examined, to prevent retrofitting.
method.composite_score: score = causal_support(0-3) x directional_concordance(0-2) x tractability(0-2) x tissue_access(0-2); nulls and discordant nodes retained and reported.
five_rung_causal_ladder: 2 Disease-state evidence in patient material and direction
3 Drug direction reproduces the protective operation
method.tissue_access_rule: expression in a tissue != access to the vulnerable cell type != adequate free drug exposure; verify the exact tissue coverage of any expression resource before a tissue claim.
method.novelty_protocol: Reframe to 'no prior proposal found under the documented search'; search standardized chemical identity + structural analogs, mechanism terms, disease synonyms, across ClinicalTrials.gov, ChEMBL, Drugs@FDA, PubMed, OpenAlex, bioRxiv/medRxiv, and patents (Google Patents/Justia/USPTO via web search).
method.compute_note: Lightweight: processed/summary-level public data and literature-reported effect sizes rather than heavy raw reprocessing.
method.connector_gaps: No DepMap connector (used published CRISPR modifier screens + Open Targets tractability). No LINCS/Connectivity-Map connector (signature-reversal used only as optional confirmation; ChEMBL directional bioactivity as the drug-direction check).

actions.detail: Created OUTPUT/run-01/presentation/{,figures}; fixed the scoring rubric, causal ladder, tissue-access rule and novelty protocol before examining any node identity.
Resolved disease ontology IDs via Open Targets/MONDO for the three principal Complex I diseases.
Enumerated the intervention landscape from ClinicalTrials.gov across Leigh/LHON/MELAS/CI-deficiency/mitochondrial disease.
ids: counts: Leigh 11, LHON 34, MELAS 33, CI-deficiency 1, mito-disease 605
interventions: Placebo 28, KH176 6, idebenone/GS010/vatiquinone 5/5/4, rAAV2-ND4 4, KL1333 3, NR/MitoQ/EPI-743 2 each, sirolimus/bezafibrate/elamipretide 1 each

actions.detail: Retrieved and read the genome-wide genetic-modifier compendium of mitochondrial dysfunction; extracted the verbatim GPX4 synthetic-sick/lethal statement and the ferroptosis (ferrostatin-1/alpha-tocopherol rescue, not zVAD) death mode; retrieved the respiratory-chain hypoxia-protection screen.
ids: PMID 31730859 (To et al., Cell 2019; PMC7053407; 191 modifiers)
PMID 29290465 (Ingold et al., Cell 2017; GPX4 needs Se+GSH)

actions.detail: Scored a candidate node universe (ferroptosis-defence axis + established mito-disease modifiers) on the frozen composite; retained nulls/discordant; the substrate-hardening (membrane-PUFA) node ranked top, GPX4-activation scored 0 (undruggable direction), SLC7A11 scored 0 (tissue-inaccessible), the ISR axis flagged discordant.
Queried GTEx v8 median TPM for the defence-axis genes in affected tissues; confirmed GTEx has no optic-nerve/retina tissue, so the RGC claim rests on iPSC-RGC/primary-retina data.
ids: GPX4: cortex 235, putamen 188, substantia nigra 225, tibial nerve 294, muscle 197, heart-LV 118 TPM

actions.detail: Located patient/model omics datasets for disease-state and penetrance evidence (verified organism/cell type/contrast at fetch).
ids: GSE144914 LHON penetrance / no bioenergetic compensation (PMID 32687992)

actions.detail: Retrieved the rat-retina rotenone model showing partial Complex I inhibition causes oxidative damage in retinal ganglion cells (LHON-relevant, but oxidative damage != ferroptosis).

Established the kinetic-isotope-effect basis: bis-allylic H-abstraction is rate-limiting in PUFA autoxidation; deuteration slows it and a sub-stoichiometric D-PUFA fraction suffices (threshold effect).
ids: PMID 25578654 (Andreyev et al., Free Radic Biol Med 2015)

actions.detail: Established in-vivo target engagement in the correct tissue: bis-allylic deuterated DHA incorporates into brain phospholipids and resists peroxidation in vivo, and reaches neural retina/RPE with oral dosing.

Collected functional-rescue evidence for deuterated PUFAs in adjacent lipid-peroxidation diseases (the compound class's efficacy signal), plus human oral bioavailability of the clinical-stage exemplar.

ids: Docosahexaenoic acid / doconexent parent: ChEMBL CHEMBL367149 (max phase 3)
linoleic acid parent PubChem CID 5280450 (InChIKey OYHQOLUKZRVURQ-HZJYTTRNSA-N); ethyl linoleate CID 5282184

actions.detail: Generated four figures (node-ranking with per-component scores; GTEx tissue-access heatmap; five-rung causal ladder with honest per-rung grades; prior-art differentiation matrix).

Verified every cited identifier by live lookup (22 PMIDs against title/journal/DOI; GEO series against organism/cell type/n; PRIDE against disease; ChEMBL IDs). Corrected two author attributions and confirmed the idebenone ~50%/NQO1 statement verbatim in its source abstract.
Ran the documented novelty search across registries and patents/preprints (see novelty).

node_ranking.formula: score = causal(0-3) x direction(0-2) x tractability(0-2) x tissue_access(0-2); nulls/discordant retained. Scores are qualitative judgments over a literature-and-data-informed candidate shortlist, not an exhaustive enumeration of the eligible universe.

nodes.node: Lipid-peroxidation substrate (membrane PUFA) hardened by bis-allylic deuteration
nodes.note: GPX4 top synthetic-lethal in the modifier screen; ferroptotic death mode; substrate-hardening is cofactor-independent (kinetic isotope effect); in-vivo brain protection shown. NB: the winning row is an inferred substrate intervention, not a node measured in the screen.
Strong causal (genome-wide screen); PHD inhibitors approved; but chronic-hypoxia systemic risk and active prior art (not novel).
Rapamycin extends Ndufs4 survival; approved; active prior art (sirolimus in trials) - not novel.
GSH-independent ferroptosis suppressor; NAD(P)H-powered (partly CI-compromised); activators nascent.
ACSL4 loss protects; inhibitors exist; low brain ACSL4, broad lipid roles -> tox risk.
Correlative; precursors approved/OTC; active prior art and weak CI-specific causal support.
The Complex-I-specific arm feeding lipid-peroxidation defence; activators early; NADPH also ETC-limited.
Direction discordant across models (protective in cardiomyopathy vs ISRIB-benefit reports); flagged, not built on.
Strongest hit and correct direction, but no drug-like activator exists (selenoprotein) -> tractability 0.
Cystine import for GSH; near-absent in affected post-mitotic tissue -> tissue_access 0.

decision.selected_compound: Deuterated docosahexaenoic acid (D-DHA): bis-allylic C-D DHA, oral ethyl ester; clinical-stage class exemplar RT001 (bis-allylic 11,11-D2-linoleic acid ethyl ester).
decision.mechanism: Substrate-level, cofactor-independent suppression of membrane lipid peroxidation via the deuterium kinetic isotope effect at the rate-limiting bis-allylic H-abstraction step; blocks ferroptotic death downstream of Complex I failure.
decision.direction: Reduce peroxidizable PUFA substrate (not activate an enzyme, not add a regenerable antioxidant).
decision.framing: A repurposing proposal: the mechanism and compound class are established/patented prior art; the contribution is the specific indication + the data-grounded repurposing rationale.
decision.indication_scope: LHON (MT-ND1/4/6), Leigh syndrome (NDUFS4 & related nuclear), plausibly Complex-I-predominant MELAS; DHA-rich post-mitotic tissue (retina/optic nerve, CNS, then heart/muscle). Not expected to rescue purely bioenergetic crises or fixed lesions.
decision.weakest_link: Rung 5 is UNTESTED in a Complex I model (functional rescue is cross-disease only); the pilot addresses exactly this.

novelty.conclusion: No prior proposal of D-DHA for Complex I disease found under the documented search. Mechanism and compound class are prior art; the specific indication and the data-grounded repurposing rationale are not.
novelty.mechanistic_novelty: LOW, not claimed - isotope-reinforced PUFAs protecting mitochondria is established (PMID 25578654; review PMID 32113652).
novelty.patent_novelty: LOW - class patented: US10052299B2 (deuterated-PUFA derivatives for oxidative-stress disorders, specification invokes cardiolipin's role in Complex I), US10154983 (bis-allylic D-PUFA method-of-use in a named neuromuscular disease), US10730821 (D-DHA synthesis). A 'mitochondrial disease' genus claim plausibly reads on this use.
novelty.indication_and_rationale_novelty: THE CONTRIBUTION - no deuterated PUFA proposed, trialled, or patented by name for LHON, Leigh, or primary Complex I deficiency.

registry_results.clinicaltrials_gov: 0 for deuterated/RT001/PUFA x (Leigh/LHON/CI-deficiency); all D-PUFA trials are other indications (FRDA NCT02445794, INAD NCT03570931, ALS NCT04762589, PSP NCT04937530)
registry_results.pubmed: 0 for deuterated fatty acid x (Leigh/Leber/complex I)
registry_results.openalex: 0 for deuterated DHA x Leber optic neuropathy (broader queries return generic ferroptosis reviews)
registry_results.patents: Mechanism + 'mitochondrial disease' genus claimed (US10052299B2 etc.); no claim naming LHON/Leigh/CI-deficiency for a D-PUFA; the in-force LHON patents are AAV gene therapy (US11352645 family, Wuhan Neurophth)
registry_results.preprints_analogs: bioRxiv/medRxiv + structural-analog terms: no D-PUFA-for-Complex-I proposal; D-DHA analogs are AMD/neurodegeneration

figures.desc: Candidate-node ranking on the composite score with the four component judgments shown; nulls/discordant retained.
GTEx v8 expression of ferroptosis-defence genes across affected tissues; SLC7A11 tissue-limited.
Five-rung causal ladder for D-DHA in Complex I disease, each rung graded honestly (PASSED/PARTIAL/UNRESOLVED/ANALOGY/UNTESTED).
Prior-art differentiation across mechanistic properties, with the 'untried in Complex I disease' column and a prior-art footnote.
